page.content: The data presented in Table 1 elucidates the enzymatic activities associated with the production of 5-HETE, a key metabolite in the arachidonic acid pathway, which has been implicated in various physiological and pathological processes. Notably, the metrics of interest, including Vehicle pmol/mg/min and ANG II pmol/mg/min, provide critical insights into the influence of different treatment conditions on 5-HETE synthesis.

page.content: The average production of 5-HETE under control conditions (Vehicle) was measured at 1.43 ± 0.50 pmol/mg/min, while the addition of ANG II resulted in a significantly elevated level of 1.85 ± 0.33 pmol/mg/min. These findings suggest that ANG II plays a pivotal role in modulating the enzymatic pathways involved in 5-HETE synthesis, potentially highlighting its importance in inflammatory responses and cardiovascular regulation. The observed increase in 5-HETE levels in the presence of ANG II further supports the hypothesis that angiotensin II may enhance arachidonic acid metabolism, thereby influencing downstream signaling pathways. Overall, these data not only reinforce the relevance of 5-HETE in biological systems but also underscore the impact of ANG II on its biosynthesis, warranting further exploration into the underlying mechanisms at play.
